items.title: Phase 0 — Make any game do something
items.body: APK browser, extraction, ELF loader, JNI shim, EGL, touch input, audio, save/load, and crash forensics are all done and confirmed on hardware — Hill Climb Racing is playable. Two items remain open: the Shop/IAP screen crash (root-caused, fix in — see the HCR items below) and confirming save persistence survives a full app restart.

items.title: Phase 2 — Stability
items.body: Real pthread/mutex/condvar/rwlock/semaphore support and ordered .so loading are done. Still open: dl_iterate_phdr for stack unwinders, real .so/JIT unload (so a second game session doesn't need an app restart), and per-channel audio pitch/rate control (needs a custom resampler — SDL_mixer has no per-channel rate API).

items.title: Phase 3 — Polish
items.body: Themed UI, About screen, reinstall button, and version-bump system are done. Still open: a per-APK settings overlay (resolution/framerate cap) and APK delete/manage from the launcher UI.

items.title: Phase 5 — Feels like real Android device APIs
items.body: Real accelerometer, gyroscope, and battery status are implemented via the real NDK Sensor API and libnx psm (untested against a live game — Hill Climb Racing uses none of them). Still open: real device info (Build.MODEL etc.) — blocked on a JNI static-field-dispatch refactor, since FindClass/GetStaticFieldID currently returns the same dummy ID for every field.

items.title: Shop/IAP screen crash
items.body: Opening the in-game Shop crashes back to the launcher. Two suspected causes fixed: a possible deadlock in the crash-forensics logger (now lock-free for this case) and isNetworkAvailable always claiming "online" regardless of real connectivity. Needs a fresh hardware test + log to confirm.

items.title: 32-bit (armeabi-v7a) game support
items.body: Running AArch32 code on Switch is possible in principle via a per-title Atmosphere address-space override, but the one real precedent project depends on a 32-bit libnx build that isn't publicly available. The launcher already detects and blocks 32-bit-only APKs with an explanation rather than attempting it.

items.title: Hill Climb Racing — UserDefaults save: durability vs. stall tradeoff
items.body: A background-thread save (to kill the ~700-900ms/save stall) was tried 2026-07-10, then reverted same day: the first real hardware test showed total save loss (every relaunch fresh), likely compounded by the still-open Shop-screen hard crash skipping the synchronous exit-time save entirely. Saves are synchronous again — durable, but the original stalls are back. Needs: (1) root-cause the Shop crash so there's always a clean exit path, (2) separately, real hardware evidence before trying an async save path again.

items.title: Hill Climb Racing — first-load Event-texture stall (~8s)
items.body: A separate, unexplained one-time ~8160ms stall at first load, logged immediately before the game's own "[fsgame] Event textures asset loaded (33/33)" line — looks like the game's own synchronous texture-atlas decode, not a compat-layer bug. Our socket layer is fully stubbed (instant ECONNREFUSED), so it isn't a network timeout. Not investigated further yet.

items.title: Idea — Mii driver support
items.body: Replace Hill Climb Racing's driver with the player's own Mii. The driver is a modular Box2D ragdoll rig (separate head/body/beard/hat pieces) that already supports full re-skins this way. Concrete path: fetch the Switch user's Mii face via libnx account's accountProfileLoadImage (a JPEG) and patch it over driver-head.png using the same asset-patch pipeline already built for the MOGA controller image.

items.title: Phase 4 — Real controller support (docked mode)
items.body: Not started — right now only touch input exists (handheld-only). Hill Climb Racing has native MOGA controller support built in (confirmed via bundled MOGA Pro/Pocket assets + save-key reads), and MOGA's face-button layout is a diagonal swap (A↔B, X↔Y) from Switch's own — mapping Joy-Con/Pro Controller input to the game's existing MOGA listener could be a real shortcut here, no game-side changes needed.

items.title: Tico emulator core ("Horizon")
items.body: Long-term direction: grow this from a single-game compat layer into a proper, reusable Android-game core for the tico frontend (github.com/orgs/ticohq), taking architectural inspiration from tico's existing emulator forks (tico-dolphin, tico-ppsspp, tico-melonds, etc.). Not started, not scoped yet.

items.title: Automated screenshot capture for the compat dataset
items.body: Capture more screenshots automatically during a test run (not just milestone frames) and fold them into what a compat-report submission includes, for better visual regression tracking across versions.

items.title: Automatic opt-in compatibility telemetry
items.body: Auto-submit compat reports from Switches connected to Wi-Fi, gated behind an explicit opt-in in Settings with real legal/privacy compliance copy — not on by default. Longer-term: a live "is this game known to work" lookup while online.

items.title: Compat-report APK integrity check
items.body: Cross-check the submitted APK's SHA-256 (already captured) against the one the attached logs were actually generated from, so a report can't silently drift from a different APK build than the one tested.

items.title: Compat-report metadata: Android Horizon build + Switch firmware + Atmosphere version
items.body: Log which Android Horizon build ran each session, plus the Switch firmware and Atmosphere versions where available. Reports submitted before this existed should render as N/A/Unknown, backfilled with the latest build available at the time of that submission rather than guessed blindly.

items.title: Compatibility list UI overhaul
items.body: Group same-package-ID submissions with different versions into an expandable dropdown; add sorting, a search bar, and separate x32/x64 tabs; fuzzy search; group games by publisher using the package-ID prefix (e.g. com.kiloo.* under Kiloo); blur PEGI-18 game images by default for a family-friendly page, looked up by package ID.

items.title: Credits — smooth auto-scroll
items.body: Replace the static credits list (in-app and on the website) with a smoothly auto-scrolling one, similar to Hill Climb Racing's own credits screen.

items.title: Documentation site redesign
items.body: Make docs read like an actual documentation site (real nav/structure/search) instead of one flat page; carry the same redesign into the rest of the website, taking cues from Bluesky's light-mode UI for readability.

items.title: Phase 1 — Touch input
items.body: Switch touchscreen mapped directly to the game's native touch calls; confirmed steering Hill Climb Racing on real hardware.
